name: case-study-house
description: Builds a 48 by 24 foot modernist case study house on a 4 by 3 structural column grid. Sets the site footprint, configures bay A as the house envelope, raises perimeter walls a half foot thick, removes the default door and window apertures, then adds six new apertures (two south windows, one north door, one north window, one east window, one west window). Defines two interior program zones, a living zone and a service zone, and enables tactile3d output with a ten foot wall height and a four foot section cut so the model prints as a three dimensional tactile floor plan. Ends with a describe command so a screen reader user hears the full model summary after replay. Note: default bays B and C remain unless removed manually; the current controller has no bay delete command, so for a pristine demo load case-study-house.state.json directly or start from an empty state before running this macro.
commands: set bay A label "Case Study House"
aperture A add w_s1 window x 0 4 8 4
aperture A add w_s2 window x 0 36 8 4
aperture A add d_n1 door x 2 6 3 7
aperture A add w_n1 window x 2 24 12 4
aperture A add w_e1 window y 3 6 12 4
aperture A add w_w1 window y 0 6 12 4
